content: Standard pattern for small authoritative multiplayer/quiz apps (see Colyseus, Ably quiz framework): server holds a single source-of-truth state object per session/room; server is modeled as a state machine that processes only server-timestamped commands in order; clients are 'dumb' renderers that emit intents (user actions) and receive full/delta state pushes, never computing authoritative state themselves. For a 4-6 team, single-room, single-server classroom app, a plain in-memory JS object (no external game framework needed) implementing an explicit phase state machine (lobby -> phase1 -> phase2 -> phase3 -> results) is sufficient; frameworks like Colyseus are overkill for this scale.
